channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Drug Safety & Pharmacovigilance
channel.description: Weekly IBD literature: Drug Safety & Pharmacovigilance

item.title: Navigating the Diagnostic Continuum: Feline Lymphoplasmacytic Enteritis and Low-Grade Intestinal T-cell Lymphoma.
item.description: The Veterinary clinics of North America. Small animal practice - Review - This article discusses the importance of accurately diagnosing feline gastrointestinal diseases, particularly differentiating inflammatory bowel disease from alimentary lymphoma. It emphasizes that...

item.title: Advances in the use of Janus kinase inhibitors.
item.description: Current opinion in rheumatology - Over the last two decades, oral small molecules that target intracellular protein kinases that mediate intracellular signaling have been found to be effective in multiple immune mediated inflammato...

item.title: Beyond drug delivery: nanoparticles as active modulators of immunometabolism for treating inflammatory diseases.
item.description: Journal of drug targeting - Review - Immunometabolism is central to chronic inflammatory diseases, with metabolic reprogramming including dysregulated glycolysis, mitochondrial dysfunction, and excessive ROS production driving patholo...

item.title: Shikonin: Unraveling its Multi-Mechanistic Anticancer and Anti- Inflammatory Mechanisms.
item.description: Current medicinal chemistry - Shikonin, a naphthoquinone secondary metabolite derived from the dried roots of Lithospermum erythrorhizon Sieb. et Zucc. (family Boraginaceae), has garnered significant attention in natural drug d...

item.title: Disseminated iatrogenic upper gastrointestinal Kaposi sarcoma following prolonged steroid use in a patients with Crohn's disease: a case report.
item.description: Journal of medical case reports - Kaposi sarcoma is a malignant vascular neoplasm caused by human herpesvirus-8. It is mostly seen in immunocompromised individuals, particularly those with human immunodeficiency virus/acquired immu...

item.title: Cutaneous Leishmaniasis in Patients under Biologic Therapies: A Spanish CLINI-AEDV Multicentre Study.
item.description: Acta dermato-venereologica - Multicenter study - Anti-TNFα agents have been associated with the reactivation of granulomatous infections, including leishmaniasis. Cases of atypical or recurrent cutaneous (CL) or mucocutaneous leishmaniasis (MCL) ...

item.title: Adherence to Recommended Immunisation Schedules for Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) on Biologics: A Retrospective Study at Our Lady's Hospital, Navan, Ireland.
item.description: Cureus - Introduction Patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), particularly those receiving biologic therapies, are at increased risk of infections due to immunosuppression. Vaccinations play a vital...

item.title: From Gut to Metabolism: Exploring the Intersection of Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Systemic Metabolic Dysfunction.
item.description: Journal of clinical gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD), comprising Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, are chronic, relapsing inflammatory disorders increasingly recognized as systemic conditions with significant e...

item.title: Once-daily oral ritlecitinib or brepocitinib versus placebo in patients with moderate-to-severely active Crohn's disease (PIZZICATO): an international, randomised, phase 2a trial.
item.description: EClinicalMedicine - Crohn's disease (CD) has a high unmet need for treatment. Ritlecitinib (Janus Kinase [JAK]3/tyrosine kinase expressed in hepatocellular carcinoma [TEC] family kinase inhibitor) or brepocitinib (tyr...

item.title: Predictors of increased acute postoperative pain after elective minimally invasive colorectal surgery.
item.description: International journal of colorectal disease - Postoperative acute pain is a major obstacle to archiving key goals in modern perioperative treatment concepts such as ERAS® (enhanced recovery after surgery). Despite a multimodal pain management ...

item.title: Beyond the Flare: A Case of Disseminated Tuberculosis and Thrombosis Masquerading as IBD Exacerbation.
item.description: Clinical journal of gastroenterology - Differentiating inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) flares from infectious complications in patients on anti-TNFα therapy presents a significant diagnostic challenge. This case report describes a 54-y...

item.title: Wenyang Decoction Ameliorates DSS-Induced Ulcerative Colitis by Regulating Macrophage Polarization and the PI3K/AKT/mTOR/HIF-1α Signaling Pathway.
item.description: Mediators of inflammation - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease characterized by intestinal inflammation and epithelial damage. This study aims to investigate the therapeutic effects of Wenyang dec...

item.title: Engineered Biomimetic Nanoparticles With Targeted Delivery of Chlorothiazide for the Treatment of Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Angewandte Chemie (International ed. in English) - While conventional treatment strategies for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) focus primarily on suppressing overactive immunity, there is still a lack of definitive interventions targeting key path...

item.title: Phthalates exposure and serum uric acid level in patients with Crohn's disease: A cross-sectional study.
item.description: PloS one - Crohn's disease (CD) is closely associated with disorders of uric acid metabolism. Our previous research found an association between phthalate exposure and oxidative stress in CD, suggesting a pot...

item.title: Integrating Human Intestinal Organoids into FDA'S New Approach Methodologies for Drug Discovery.
item.description: Advanced science (Weinheim, Baden-Wurttemberg, Germany) - Review - Reliance on cell lines and animal models for toxicity testing has long been the cornerstone of preclinical drug discovery for intestinal diseases, but the physiology, genetics, and disease etiology...

item.title: Mechanisms and therapeutic potential of traditional Chinese medicine for inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: World journal of gastroenterology - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic relapsing inflammatory disorder of the intestine with a rising global incidence that significantly impairs patients' quality of life. Although modern m...

item.title: Pharmacokinetics, Safety, and Tolerability of GS-1427, an Oral Prodrug of a Potent and Selective α4β7 Integrin Inhibitor, in Healthy Participants.
item.description: Journal of clinical pharmacology - RCT - GS-1427 is the oral prodrug of GS-1069518, an inhibitor of the α4β7 integrin, and is currently in development for the treatment of ulcerative colitis (UC). This first-in-human, phase 1, placebo-con...

item.title: Association of Geography With Screening and Development of Cervical Neoplasia in Those With Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - While cancer risk is elevated in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), results are less clear for cervical cancer. Screening has made cervical cancer relatively preventable, but geography impacts acces...

item.title: Therapeutic Potential of Spermidine in Rheumatoid Arthritis: Mechanisms and Future Directions.
item.description: International journal of rheumatic diseases - Review - Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) is a chronic autoimmune disease that not only severely impairs joint function but also leads to various systemic complications, significantly reducing patients' quality of...

item.title: Safety and Tolerability of Xanthohumol in Adults With Crohn's Disease: Results of a Triple-Masked, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Phase 2 Trial.
item.description: Molecular nutrition & food research - RCT - Xanthohumol (XN), a flavonoid from hops (Humulus lupulus), exhibits mucosal anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and microbiome-modulating effects, making it a candidate therapeutic for inflammatory bow...

item.title: Cutaneous Manifestations and Dermatologic Adverse Events in IBD: A Clinical Update.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Review - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs), including Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, are immune-mediated disorders characterized by chronic gastrointestinal inflammation and a broad spectrum of ex...

item.title: Early Development of Ocadusertib, a Selective Receptor-Interacting Serine/Threonine-Protein Kinase 1 Inhibitor.
item.description: Clinical and translational science - RCT - Receptor-interacting serine/threonine-protein kinase 1 (RIPK1) inhibitors are being investigated for chronic inflammatory diseases such as rheumatoid arthritis, inflammatory bowel disease, and psor...

item.title: Hepatitis E Infection in Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Diseases: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Journal of viral hepatitis - Meta-analysis - Opportunistic infections are increasingly recognised in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), particularly among those receiving immunosuppressive therapy. Hepatitis E virus (HEV) is a le...

item.title: The Costs of Inflammatory Bowel Disease and the Financial Impact of Insurance Delays.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) are chronic gastrointestinal disorders with systemic manifestations for which highly effective therapies and better understanding of treatment targets have transfo...

item.title: Exploring the potential of chitosan-α-lipoic acid copolymer/sodium alginate-coated hollow zein nanoparticles for oral astaxanthin delivery: Structural characterization, safety assessment and anti-ulcerative colitis effect.
item.description: International journal of biological macromolecules - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease characterized by damage to the colonic mucosal barrier, persistent inflammation, and gut microbiota dysbiosis. Nevertheless, a major ...

item.title: Engineering bioavailability: Polysaccharide-protein-lipid delivery platforms for postbiotic therapeutics in gastrointestinal and systemic disorders.
item.description: International journal of biological macromolecules - Review - Postbiotics, inanimate microorganisms and their components that confer health benefits, represent a promising therapeutic class with superior stability and safety profiles compared to live probioti...

item.title: Applicator preferences for subcutaneous biologics in inflammatory bowel disease: influence on patient satisfaction and willingness to switch treatment.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - Subcutaneous biologics administered with various applicator types are widely used to treat inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). The aim was to determine the applicator characteristics most important t...

item.title: The ovarian tumor domain-containing protein 4 (OTUD4)-targeted ferroptosis inhibitor Maclekarpine E attenuates ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease driven by oxidative stress. Current therapies, including aminosalicylates and immunosuppressants, are limited by adverse effects. Nat...

item.title: Intrarectal delivery of chitosan hydrogel-encapsulated mesenchymal stem cell-conditioned media attenuates disease severity in experimental ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Inflammopharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory disease characterized by mucosal immune dysregulation and epithelial injury. While mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) hold regenerative and immunomodula...

item.title: Gut-liver axis dysregulation in colitis underlies structure-dependent pharmacokinetics of a traditional Chinese medicine.
item.description: Pharmacological research - Pharmacokinetic (PK) variability under pathological conditions poses challenges for drug efficacy and safety. Although clinical data comparing PK in healthy individuals and patients is limited, evi...

item.title: The rate and risk factors for malignancy in patients with inflammatory bowel disease in Crete: a case control study.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - About 30% of the patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) develop malignancies, which constitute the second cause of death, after cardiovascular diseases. This study aimed to investigate the ...

item.title: Discovery and structure-activity relationship analyses of 1,2-diphenylethane derivatives as a new class of GPR68 antagonists and the therapeutic effect in an inflammatory bowel disease model.
item.description: European journal of medicinal chemistry - G protein-coupled receptor 68 (GPR68), a proton-sensing GPCR, has emerged as a key player in inflammatory diseases. Its expression is substantially upregulated in the inflamed intestinal mucosa of ...

item.title: Immunogenicity and safety of a recombinant spike protein COVID vaccine in patients with inflammatory bowel disease and transplant recipient.
item.description: Vaccine - Immunosuppressed individuals are at increased risk of coronavirus disease (COVID-19). Although mRNA vaccines have shown efficacy in these populations, data on protein-based vaccines remain limited....

item.title: Design, semisynthesis, and anti-inflammatory activity evaluation of a terphenyllin compound library for alleviating ulcerative colitis.
item.description: European journal of medicinal chemistry - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic idiopathic enteritis, seriously affecting patients' quality of life and significantly increasing the risk of cancer. We screened a marine natural product-derive...

item.title: First detection of natural co-infection with mutated very virulent IBDV (mvvIBDV) and novel variant IBDV (nVarIBDV).
item.description: Poultry science - Infectious bursal disease (IBD), a globally important immunosuppressive disease of poultry caused by infectious bursal disease virus (IBDV), continues to pose a substantial threat to the poultry in...

item.title: Effectiveness and Tolerability of Anti-Tumor Necrosis Factor Alpha Therapy in Refractory Intestinal Behçet's Disease: A Large Single-Center Study.
item.description: Gut and liver - Intestinal Behçet's disease (BD) is a rare, chronic intestinal vascular disorder often refractory to conventional therapy. We aimed to assess the effectiveness and tolerability of anti-tumor necros...

item.title: Tuberculosis and inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Gastroenterologia y hepatologia - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), which includes ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease, carries an increased risk of tuberculosis (TB), particularly in patients treated with immunosuppressive ther...

item.title: Fusobacterium nucleatum: ecology, pathogenesis and clinical implications.
item.description: Nature reviews. Microbiology - Review - Fusobacterium nucleatum, a commensal microorganism and opportunistic pathogen (a pathobiont), has emerged as a key player in both local and systemic diseases. In humans, F. nucleatum is found in th...

item.title: Localized Stage Plasmablastic Lymphoma with Ulcerative Colitis in Long-term Remission after Chemoradiotherapy.
item.description: Internal medicine (Tokyo, Japan) - Case report - Plasmablastic lymphoma (PBL) is a rare and aggressive B-cell lymphoma with a poor prognosis and is typically linked to human immunodeficiency virus (HIV)-related immunodeficiency. PBL associated wi...

item.title: Comparative Safety of Janus Kinase Inhibitors vs Tumor Necrosis Factor Antagonists in Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Diseases.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - We conducted a retrospective cohort study comparing the safety of Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors vs tumor necrosis factor-a (TNF) antagonists in patients with inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs). Usi...
